open_questions.question: Synthetic policy/manual PDF corpus generation approach is unspecified: what tool/library renders the PDFs (e.g. reportlab/weasyprint/pandoc), and is the underlying text hand-authored or LLM-generated then rendered? This determines the concrete implementation of the '5.1.2 synthetic corpus generation' subtask.

Ground-truth topic/query finalization process is unspecified: who curates/verifies the ~30-50 predefined ground-truth topics and their deliberate cross-topic references (manual authorship vs. LLM-assisted drafting + human review), and what format do topic/query labels take for the recall/precision harness?

Demo deployment target is unspecified beyond 'small VM/container': which specific host/provider (e.g. a particular cloud VM, Fly.io, Railway, self-hosted box) and container orchestration (plain Docker Compose vs. something else), including domain/TLS requirements if any.

subtasks.title: Catalog record struct + fixed-size binary encode/decode
subtasks.ac: CatalogRecord{fileID, pathHash, currentVersion, sizeBytes, status, redirectTargetIDs, parentTopicID, lastModified} encodes to and decodes from a fixed-size byte layout with no data loss for all fields, including empty/zero redirectTargetIDs.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/catalog/... -run TestRecordEncodeDecode -race covering round-trip encode->decode equality for populated and zero-value records.

subtasks.title: Slotted 4KB page implementation (header, slot array, insert/read/delete slot, free-space tracking)
subtasks.ac: A page supports InsertSlot/ReadSlot/DeleteSlot within a 4KB buffer, tracks free space correctly, and rejects inserts that would overflow the page.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/catalog/... -run TestSlottedPage -race: insert until full then assert overflow error; delete then reinsert reuses freed slot space.

subtasks.title: Catalog file manager: .meta/catalog.dat open/create + page allocation + free-list page
subtasks.ac: Opening a non-existent catalog.dat creates it with an initial free-list page; allocating a new page marks it used in the free-list; deleting/merging a slot returns its page to the free-list for reclamation.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/catalog/... -run TestCatalogFileManager -race: create fresh catalog file, allocate N pages, delete some records, verify free-list reclaims and reuses page slots on next allocation.

subtasks.title: Monotonic fileID allocator persisted across restarts
subtasks.ac: fileID is an atomically-incrementing counter with no reuse/gaps semantics that matter; after process restart, the next allocated fileID continues strictly after the highest previously allocated ID.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/catalog/... -run TestFileIDAllocator -race: allocate concurrently from many goroutines and assert all IDs unique; simulate restart (reopen file manager) and assert monotonicity preserved.

subtasks.title: Catalog CRUD API with striped-mutex layer (~256 stripes hashed by fileID)
subtasks.ac: Get/Put/Delete-by-fileID API exists; two fileIDs hashing to different stripes never block each other (verified by holding one stripe's lock and confirming an operation on a different stripe proceeds without waiting).
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/catalog/... -run TestStripedLocking -race: verify no cross-stripe blocking and correct CRUD results under single-threaded and light-concurrency (functional, not the full race-test suite - that lands in Epic 2A once MVCC/split exercise it more heavily).

subtasks.title: On-disk B+Tree node layout + serialization at index/name.idx
subtasks.ac: Leaf and internal node formats are defined and serialize/deserialize round-trip losslessly; index/name.idx file is created on first use.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/btree/... -run TestNodeSerialization -race: encode/decode leaf and internal nodes with varying key counts, assert equality.

subtasks.title: Point lookup (path -> fileID)
subtasks.ac: Lookup returns the correct fileID for an inserted path and a defined not-found result for an absent path.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/btree/... -run TestLookup: insert fixed set of paths, assert lookup correctness for present and absent keys.

subtasks.title: Insert with node splitting on overflow
subtasks.ac: Inserting keys beyond a node's capacity triggers a correct split (median promoted to parent, keys partitioned correctly); tree remains balanced and all previously inserted keys remain lookup-able after many splits.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/btree/... -run TestInsertSplit: insert enough keys to force multiple levels of splitting, assert full-tree lookup correctness afterward and structural invariants (sorted keys, correct fanout).

subtasks.title: Delete with simplified rebalancing (merge-or-tombstone strategy, documented choice)
subtasks.ac: Deleting a key removes it from lookups; tree remains internally consistent (no orphaned child pointers) after interleaved insert/delete sequences.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/btree/... -run TestDelete: interleaved insert/delete sequence, assert deleted keys absent, remaining keys correct, and tree passes a structural-invariant checker.

subtasks.title: Prefix scan (list topic subtree)
subtasks.ac: Prefix scan over a topic path (e.g. `auth/`) returns exactly the set of inserted paths sharing that prefix, in sorted order.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/btree/... -run TestPrefixScan: insert a mixed set of topic paths, assert prefix scan returns exact expected subset.

subtasks.title: Persisted reload correctness test (disk round-trip)
subtasks.ac: A tree built, written to index/name.idx, closed, and reopened yields identical lookup/prefix-scan results as before closing.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/btree/... -run TestPersistReload: build tree, close, reopen file, assert lookup/prefix-scan parity.

subtasks.title: Append-only WAL segment writer + segment rotation
subtasks.ac: Log records append to wal/wal-<segment>.log; a new segment is created once the current one exceeds a configured size, with no record ever split across segments.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/wal/... -run TestSegmentWriter -race: write enough records to force rotation, assert segment boundaries and record integrity.

subtasks.title: WAL record types for catalog/index mutations + fsync-before-apply write path
subtasks.ac: A mutation is durably fsynced to the WAL before the caller's Append call returns, and before the corresponding in-memory/on-disk state change is considered committed.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/wal/... -run TestFsyncBeforeApply -race: assert WAL append completes (and fsync observed) prior to a simulated apply callback firing.

subtasks.title: Checkpoint manifest (manifest.json) tracking checkpoint pointer
subtasks.ac: Checkpointing records the WAL offset up to which state is durably applied; segments fully before the checkpoint pointer are eligible for truncation/archival.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/wal/... -run TestCheckpointManifest: write records, checkpoint, assert manifest.json reflects correct offset and old segments are archivable.

subtasks.title: Recovery replay from last checkpoint pointer forward
subtasks.ac: On startup, replay reapplies every logged-but-not-yet-checkpointed mutation, in order, exactly once, and is a no-op if the checkpoint pointer already covers all segments.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/wal/... -run TestRecoveryReplay: pre-populate WAL with mutations past the checkpoint, run recovery, assert final state matches applying the same mutations directly.

subtasks.title: Crash-injection recovery test (mid-write crash simulation)
subtasks.ac: Simulating a crash after a partial/torn record write does not corrupt recovery; the torn record is detected and discarded, and recovery proceeds from the last valid record.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/wal/... -run TestCrashInjectionRecovery -race: truncate/corrupt the tail of a segment mid-record, run recovery, assert clean state with no panic and no corrupted-record replay.

tasks.title: Single-version .md content read/write (engine/catalog/ content I/O, pre-MVCC)

subtasks.title: Content store layout: content/<fileID>.v1.md create/write path, WAL-before-apply
subtasks.ac: Creating a new topic file writes content/<fileID>.v1.md and logs the corresponding catalog mutation to the WAL before the file is considered committed.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/catalog/... -run TestContentCreate -race: create file, assert WAL record precedes catalog visibility, assert content bytes on disk match input.

subtasks.title: Full-file read path (single-threaded, no snapshot semantics yet)
subtasks.ac: Reading a file by fileID returns its current full markdown content exactly as last written.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/catalog/... -run TestContentRead: write then read, assert byte-for-byte equality.

subtasks.title: Append path (PutSegment-equivalent) + sizeBytes update + threshold-check stub
subtasks.ac: Appending markdown content to an existing file updates its stored content and the catalog's sizeBytes field; if resulting size exceeds the configured split threshold (~8KB/~2000 tokens default), a threshold-crossing signal is emitted (actual auto-split execution is out of scope until Epic 2B).
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/catalog/... -run TestContentAppend: append repeatedly, assert sizeBytes tracks content length and threshold signal fires exactly once when crossing the configured limit.

subtasks.title: Durability round-trip test across simulated restart
subtasks.ac: After writing/appending content and simulating a process restart (reopen WAL + catalog + content store), a read returns the same content as before restart.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/catalog/... -run TestContentDurabilityRestart -race: write/append, close all stores, reopen, replay WAL, assert read parity.

tasks.title: Single-threaded end-to-end integration & smoke test
modules: engine/ (cross-package integration test, no new package)

subtasks.title: Cross-package integration test: catalog + btree + wal + content, single-threaded happy path
subtasks.ac: A single test wires catalog, btree, wal, and content together to create several topic files, append to them, look them up by path, and read them back, all single-threaded, with correct results end-to-end.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/... -run TestStorageCoreIntegration -race (single-threaded workload): create N files via btree-insert+catalog+content, append, prefix-scan, full-read, assert consistency across all four modules.

subtasks.title: End-to-end crash-recovery integration test across all four modules
subtasks.ac: Killing the process mid-append (simulated) and restarting reconstructs a consistent catalog+btree+content state via WAL replay, with no partial/corrupted file visible.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/... -run TestStorageCoreCrashRecovery -race: interrupt an append mid-way, reopen all stores, run WAL recovery, assert consistent end state across catalog/btree/content.

epics.title: Phase 2a: Concurrency primitives (MVCC + striped locking + latch-crabbing B+Tree + epoch GC)

subtasks.title: Version file writer: content/<fileID>.vN.md creation with monotonic version numbering
subtasks.ac: Each write creates a new immutable version file with N strictly increasing per fileID; prior version files are left untouched.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/mvcc/... -run TestVersionWriter -race: perform sequential and concurrent writes to the same fileID, assert version numbers are strictly increasing with no collisions.

subtasks.title: Atomic CAS swap of catalog's current-version pointer post-durable-write
subtasks.ac: The catalog's currentVersion field is updated via CAS only after the new version file is durably written; a failed CAS (concurrent writer won) retries without corrupting state.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/mvcc/... -run TestCurrentVersionCAS -race: concurrent writers race to CAS, assert exactly the expected final currentVersion and no lost updates.

subtasks.title: Snapshot-read path: capture pointer at request start, read that version to completion
subtasks.ac: A reader that starts before a concurrent write commits continues to see its originally-snapshotted version for the entire read, even after the pointer advances.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/mvcc/... -run TestSnapshotRead -race: start a read, trigger a concurrent write mid-read, assert the read completes against the pre-write version content.

subtasks.title: WAL integration for version-pointer CAS
subtasks.ac: Every version-pointer CAS is logged to the WAL before being applied, consistent with the catalog-mutation invariant in wal.md.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/mvcc/... -run TestVersionCASWAL -race: assert WAL record precedes pointer visibility; crash-inject mid-CAS and confirm recovery reconstructs a valid pointer.

subtasks.title: Concurrent writer/reader race test (no torn reads)
subtasks.ac: Many concurrent writers and readers against the same fileID never produce a torn read (partial/mixed-version content) and never lose a committed write.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/mvcc/... -race -run TestConcurrentReadersWriters: N writer goroutines + M reader goroutines against shared fileIDs, assert every read is a complete single version's content.

subtasks.title: Snapshot epoch refcounting (increment on start, decrement on completion)
subtasks.ac: Each snapshot increments its epoch's refcount on creation and decrements on completion; refcounts never go negative and reach zero once all referencing readers finish.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/mvcc/... -run TestEpochRefcount -race: open/close overlapping snapshots across epochs, assert refcount bookkeeping is correct at every step.

subtasks.title: Background compactor reclaiming eligible old versions
subtasks.ac: A version file is deleted only once its epoch's refcount is zero and it is not the current version; the current version is never reclaimed regardless of refcount.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/mvcc/... -run TestCompactor -race: create several old versions, hold some snapshots open, run compactor, assert only eligible versions are removed.

subtasks.title: GC correctness test under concurrent reader/writer/compactor load
subtasks.ac: Running writers, readers, and the compactor concurrently never reclaims a version still referenced by an in-flight snapshot.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/mvcc/... -race -run TestGCUnderConcurrency: long-running readers holding old snapshots concurrently with writers advancing versions and the compactor running, assert no active snapshot's version is ever deleted.

subtasks.title: Concurrent stress test across many fileIDs verifying stripe isolation
subtasks.ac: Many goroutines performing Put/Get/Delete across many distinct fileIDs complete with correct final state and no lock contention across unrelated stripes causing incorrect results.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/catalog/... -race -run TestStripedConcurrencyStress: high-goroutine-count CRUD workload across a wide fileID range, assert final catalog state matches a serial-execution oracle.

subtasks.title: Contention benchmark: striped mutex vs. single global-lock baseline
subtasks.ac: A benchmark demonstrates the striped-mutex implementation sustains meaningfully higher concurrent throughput than a naive single-global-lock baseline under many-fileID contention.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/catalog/... -bench BenchmarkStripedVsGlobalLock -benchmem: report throughput numbers for both implementations side by side.

subtasks.title: Per-node latch (mutex) + version counter fields added to node layout
subtasks.ac: Every node carries a latch and a version counter that increments on any structural mutation to that node.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/btree/... -run TestNodeLatchFields -race: mutate a node, assert its version counter increments exactly once per mutation.

subtasks.title: Latch-crabbing insert (lock parent, lock child, release parent, propagate splits)
subtasks.ac: Concurrent inserts into disjoint subtrees proceed without blocking each other; inserts into the same subtree remain correct and no writer ever holds more than a parent+child latch pair at once.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/btree/... -race -run TestCrabbingInsert: concurrent inserts across disjoint and overlapping subtrees, assert final tree contains all inserted keys with correct structure.

subtasks.title: Latch-crabbing delete with lock hand-over
subtasks.ac: Concurrent deletes correctly remove keys without corrupting sibling/parent pointers, using the same crabbing discipline as insert.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/btree/... -race -run TestCrabbingDelete: concurrent deletes interleaved with concurrent inserts, assert final tree matches a serial-execution oracle.

subtasks.title: Optimistic lock-free read path with version-counter retry
subtasks.ac: A reader never blocks a writer or another reader; if a node's version counter changed mid-read, the reader retries and eventually returns a consistent result.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/btree/... -race -run TestOptimisticRead: concurrent readers running alongside writers that mutate the exact nodes being read, assert every read either returns a consistent snapshot or retries (no torn reads observed).

subtasks.title: Full concurrent race-test suite (mixed insert/delete/lookup, disjoint + overlapping subtrees)
subtasks.ac: Under `go test -race`, a mixed workload of many concurrent inserters, deleters, and readers across both disjoint and overlapping subtrees produces a final tree state matching a serial-execution oracle, with zero data races reported.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/btree/... -race -run TestConcurrentMixedWorkload -count=5 (repeat runs to catch flakiness): assert zero race detector reports and oracle-matching final state across all 5 runs.

subtasks.title: Size-threshold detection hook on the append path (default ~8KB/~2000 tokens, tunable)
subtasks.ac: An append that pushes a file's sizeBytes over the configured threshold triggers exactly one split-eligibility signal; appends that stay under threshold trigger none.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/split/... -run TestThresholdDetection: append content crossing/not-crossing the threshold in various increments, assert signal fires only on the crossing append.

subtasks.title: Per-file CAS `splitInProgress` flag ensuring exactly one split wins per threshold crossing
subtasks.ac: When many goroutines concurrently trigger a threshold crossing on the same file, exactly one CAS succeeds and initiates the split; all others observe the flag already set and back off without initiating a duplicate split.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/split/... -race -run TestSplitInProgressCAS: many goroutines racing to CAS the flag on the same fileID, assert exactly one winner via atomic counter of split-initiation calls.

subtasks.title: Mark file SPLITTING in catalog; queue new writers; verify existing readers unaffected via MVCC
subtasks.ac: Once a split begins, the catalog record transitions to SPLITTING, new writer requests to that file are queued rather than applied, and readers holding a pre-split MVCC snapshot continue to see the old version undisturbed.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/split/... -race -run TestSplittingStatusIsolation: start a split, assert new writes queue, assert an in-flight reader's snapshot content is unaffected by the SPLITTING transition.

subtasks.title: Define local `SplitProposer` interface decoupling split from the real ProposeSplit RPC transport
subtasks.ac: `engine/split/` depends only on a `SplitProposer` interface (ProposeSplit(fileContent) -> plan); no direct gRPC/agent dependency exists yet (real gRPC wiring happens in Epic 3 once proto/ and agents/ingestion exist).
subtasks.test: go build ./engine/split/... succeeds with zero imports of a gRPC client package; go test ./engine/split/... -run TestSplitProposerInterface asserts the interface contract via a trivial fake.

subtasks.title: Deterministic mock SplitProposer with fixture split plans
subtasks.ac: A test-only mock implementation returns fixed, deterministic `[{newPath, sectionRanges}]` + redirect-summary fixtures, unblocking split-sequence testing before the real Python ProposeSplit exists.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/split/... -run TestMockSplitProposer: assert the mock returns the expected fixed plan for known fixture input.

tasks.title: Atomic split-transaction execution (engine/split/, engine/graph/ minimal writer)

subtasks.title: Allocate new fileIDs + write new .md files for split-off content per the split plan
subtasks.ac: For each `{newPath, sectionRanges}` entry, a new fileID is allocated and a new content file is written containing exactly the specified section range's content.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/split/... -run TestSplitAllocateAndWrite: given a fixture plan, assert new files exist with correct content matching the specified section ranges.

subtasks.title: Write redirect/stub at old path + update catalog entries (status SPLIT/REDIRECT, redirectTargetIDs)
subtasks.ac: After split, the original file's catalog record has status REDIRECT (or SPLIT, per the two-step transition) with redirectTargetIDs pointing at the new fileIDs, and a stub file replaces the original content at the old path.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/split/... -run TestSplitRedirectStub: run a split, assert old-path catalog record's status/redirectTargetIDs and stub file content.

subtasks.title: Insert new topic paths into B+Tree; repoint old path's entry to the redirect stub
subtasks.ac: Each new topic path resolves via B+Tree lookup to its new fileID; the old path still resolves, but now to the redirect-stub fileID rather than being deleted.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/split/... -run TestSplitBtreeRepoint: run a split, assert btree lookups for new paths and the repointed old path.

subtasks.title: Minimal graph edge-append primitive (engine/graph/) sufficient for SPLIT_SIBLING/REDIRECT edges
subtasks.ac: A minimal append-only edge writer exists supporting adding `{targetFileID, edgeType, weight, lastUpdated}` edges for at least SPLIT_SIBLING and REDIRECT edge types (full CSR storage/compaction/traversal API is deferred to Epic 3's graph store task).
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/graph/... -run TestMinimalEdgeAppend: append edges of both types, assert they are retrievable by source fileID.

subtasks.title: Add SPLIT_SIBLING edges between new files; re-point inbound edges to the redirect stub
subtasks.ac: All pairs of newly split-off files gain a SPLIT_SIBLING edge; any edge that previously pointed at the old path is retargeted to the redirect stub rather than rewriting the full inbound-edge list.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/split/... -run TestSplitGraphEdges: run a split with pre-existing inbound edges to the old path, assert SPLIT_SIBLING edges among new files and inbound edges now pointing at the stub.

subtasks.title: Commit entire split as a single WAL-covered, fsynced transaction; release queued writers on commit
subtasks.ac: All of allocation, content writes, catalog updates, btree updates, and graph edge writes commit atomically under one WAL-covered transaction that is fsynced before the split becomes visible; writers queued during SPLITTING are released only after commit.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/split/... -race -run TestSplitAtomicCommit: crash-inject mid-transaction, assert recovery yields either the pre-split or fully-post-split state, never a partial split.

subtasks.title: Invalidate markdown header-offset cache atomically within the same split/append transaction
subtasks.ac: Any transaction that changes file boundaries (split or append) invalidates the affected file's header-offset cache in the same atomic transaction, so `ReadPartial` never serves offsets against stale content.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/split/... -race -run TestSectionIndexInvalidation: perform a split, then immediately issue a ReadPartial-style offset read against the old and new files, assert offsets reflect post-split content only.

tasks.title: Auto-split concurrent race-test suite (mandatory highest-risk gate)

subtasks.title: Many-goroutine concurrent-append race test: no data loss, exactly-one split, no dangling edges
subtasks.ac: Many goroutines append to the same file simultaneously, crossing the split threshold multiple times in aggregate; the test asserts no appended data is lost, exactly one split executes per threshold crossing, and no graph edge references a nonexistent fileID afterward.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/split/... -race -run TestConcurrentAppendSplitRace -count=10: assert content completeness, split-count invariant, and graph-edge referential integrity across repeated runs.

subtasks.title: Reader-during-split test: pre-split MVCC snapshot readers see consistent old content throughout
subtasks.ac: A reader that snapshots the file immediately before a split begins continues to read fully consistent pre-split content for the duration of its read, regardless of the split completing concurrently.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/split/... -race -run TestReaderDuringSplit: start a long read, trigger a concurrent split, assert the reader's returned content matches the pre-split snapshot exactly.

tasks.title: Full graph adjacency store + traversal API (engine/graph/)

subtasks.title: CSR-like compact adjacency array format persisted to graph.dat
subtasks.ac: Adjacency data for all fileIDs persists to graph.dat in a CSR-like compact array format that reloads correctly after a process restart.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/graph/... -run TestCSRFormat: write adjacency data, reopen graph.dat, assert identical adjacency on reload.

subtasks.title: Append-only per-node edge log writer (avoids locking a shared adjacency array)
subtasks.ac: Concurrent edge appends to different source fileIDs never block each other because each writes to its own per-node log rather than a shared array.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/graph/... -race -run TestPerNodeEdgeLog: concurrent appenders across many distinct fileIDs, assert no cross-blocking and correct per-node log contents.

subtasks.title: Periodic compaction of edge logs into the compacted CSR adjacency array
subtasks.ac: Compaction merges accumulated per-node edge-log entries into the CSR array without losing or duplicating edges, including weight increments on repeated ENTITY_COOCCUR edges.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/graph/... -run TestCompaction: append many edges including repeated ENTITY_COOCCUR pairs, compact, assert resulting CSR array has correct merged/weighted edges.

subtasks.title: Edge-type support: ENTITY_COOCCUR (weighted), LLM_ASSERTED, SPLIT_SIBLING, REDIRECT
subtasks.ac: All four edge types can be created, weighted (for ENTITY_COOCCUR), and filtered on by type in traversal queries.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/graph/... -run TestEdgeTypes: create one edge of each type, assert type-filtered queries return the correct subset.

subtasks.title: GraphNeighbors(fileID, depth, edgeTypeFilter, maxNodes) traversal API, hard-capped at k+2k
subtasks.ac: Traversal expands 0-2 hops from a source fileID, respects the edgeTypeFilter, and never returns more than maxNodes (system-wide default k+2k) results even if more are reachable.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/graph/... -run TestGraphNeighbors: build a graph with >maxNodes reachable nodes within 2 hops, assert traversal result size is capped at maxNodes and respects the type filter.

subtasks.title: Graph correctness test: full insert/traversal/compaction round trip
subtasks.ac: A combined workload of edge inserts, compaction, and traversal queries produces results consistent with a serial-execution oracle.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/graph/... -run TestGraphRoundTrip: interleave inserts/compaction/traversal, assert oracle-matching results at each checkpoint.

tasks.title: proto/ gRPC contracts + engine/rpc/ server + real ProposeSplit wiring

subtasks.title: Define shared .proto files (PutSegment, GetFile, ReadPartial, GraphNeighbors, SearchCandidates, ProposeSplit)
subtasks.ac: A single set of .proto files under proto/ defines request/response messages and service methods for all six RPCs, compiling cleanly with protoc for both Go and Python targets.
subtasks.test: protoc --go_out=. --go-grpc_out=. proto/*.proto and the Python grpc_tools.protoc equivalent both succeed with zero errors; generated stub signatures match the documented RPC list in docs/LLD/rpc.md.

subtasks.title: Generate Go stubs; implement engine/rpc/ server for PutSegment/GetFile/ReadPartial/GraphNeighbors/SearchCandidates
subtasks.ac: Each RPC's Go server implementation delegates to the correct underlying module (catalog/content for PutSegment+GetFile+ReadPartial, graph for GraphNeighbors, btree for SearchCandidates) and returns correct results for a fixture request.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/rpc/... -run TestRPCServerHandlers: issue each RPC against a fixture-populated store, assert responses match direct module calls.

subtasks.title: Generate Python stubs; wire engine/split/'s SplitProposer to call real ProposeSplit gRPC (replacing Epic 2b's mock)
subtasks.ac: engine/split/ can be configured to use a real gRPC-backed SplitProposer implementation that calls the Python agent service's ProposeSplit RPC instead of the deterministic mock from task-2b.2.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/split/... -run TestGRPCSplitProposer (with a local test gRPC server standing in for the agent service): assert the real client correctly marshals requests and unmarshals the plan response.

subtasks.title: Per-call latency/cost interceptors on both Go and Python gRPC sides
subtasks.ac: Every RPC call logs per-call latency on both sides, and the Python side additionally logs LLM token cost where applicable, in a format the future benchmark harness (Epic 5) can consume.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/rpc/... -run TestLatencyInterceptor and a corresponding pytest for the Python interceptor: assert a latency record is emitted for every call.

subtasks.title: gRPC integration test: engine <-> agent round trip for ProposeSplit and PutSegment
subtasks.ac: A real end-to-end gRPC call from the Go engine to a running Python agent service correctly executes ProposeSplit and PutSegment with matching request/response semantics.
subtasks.test: An integration test (Go test or a scripted smoke test) starts the Python gRPC server, issues ProposeSplit and PutSegment from the Go client, asserts expected responses.

subtasks.title: PDF normalizer via pymupdf -> plain text with page markers
subtasks.ac: Given a PDF file, the normalizer produces plain text with page-boundary markers preserved and no page content dropped.
subtasks.test: pytest agents/ingestion/test_normalize_pdf.py: run against a fixture PDF, assert output text contains expected per-page markers and content.

subtasks.title: Email normalizer (Enron-specific) -> sender/subject/thread/body fields
subtasks.ac: Given a raw Enron-format email file, the normalizer extracts sender, subject, thread id, and body correctly.
subtasks.test: pytest agents/ingestion/test_normalize_email.py: run against fixture Enron emails, assert extracted fields match expected values.

subtasks.title: Support-ticket normalizer (structured JSON/CSV) -> labeled text blob
subtasks.ac: Given a structured support-ticket record (JSON or CSV row), the normalizer produces a labeled text blob preserving all structured fields as labeled sections.
subtasks.test: pytest agents/ingestion/test_normalize_ticket.py: run against fixture ticket records, assert labeled output matches expected format.

subtasks.title: Common RawDocument record + normalizer dispatch by sourceType
subtasks.ac: A single RawDocument{id, sourceType, text, structuredFields, timestamp} record type is produced by all three normalizers, and a dispatch function selects the correct normalizer given a sourceType.
subtasks.test: pytest agents/ingestion/test_dispatch.py: feed one fixture of each sourceType through the dispatcher, assert correct normalizer invoked and RawDocument shape is consistent across all three.

tasks.title: Segmentation agent + ProposeSplit + LLM client (agents/ingestion/, agents/llm/)

subtasks.title: LLMClient protocol/ABC + Ollama implementation (agents/llm/)
subtasks.ac: A common LLMClient interface is defined; an Ollama-backed implementation (default e.g. Llama 3.1 8B) satisfies it and no other agent module calls the Ollama SDK directly.
subtasks.test: pytest agents/llm/test_ollama_client.py: mock the Ollama HTTP call, assert LLMClient.complete()-style call shape and response parsing.

subtasks.title: Candidate topic shortlisting: local embedding pre-filter + btree SearchCandidates
subtasks.ac: Given a document, the shortlister returns a bounded candidate topic list (not the full catalog) using a cheap embedding/BM25 pre-filter against SearchCandidates, reducing segmentation prompt size and topic-name drift.
subtasks.test: pytest agents/ingestion/test_shortlist.py (SearchCandidates mocked): assert shortlist size is bounded and relevant fixture topics are included ahead of irrelevant ones.

subtasks.title: Segmentation prompt + structured JSON output parsing
subtasks.ac: Given document text + shortlist, the segmentation call returns and correctly parses `{topic_action, target_topic, new_topic_path, content_markdown, entities, related_topics}` for each segment, rejecting malformed LLM output with a clear error.
subtasks.test: pytest agents/ingestion/test_segment.py (LLMClient mocked with fixture responses, including one malformed-response case): assert correct parse and correct error handling.

subtasks.title: Wiring: execute segments via PutSegment; entities -> ENTITY_COOCCUR edges; related_topics -> LLM_ASSERTED edges
subtasks.ac: Each parsed segment's APPEND_EXISTING/CREATE_NEW action is executed via the engine's PutSegment RPC; entities feed entity.idx and increment ENTITY_COOCCUR edge weights; related_topics create LLM_ASSERTED edges.
subtasks.test: pytest agents/ingestion/test_segment_wiring.py (engine RPC client mocked): assert correct PutSegment calls and correct edge-creation calls for a fixture segment set.

subtasks.title: Real ProposeSplit implementation (replaces Epic 2b's deterministic mock)
subtasks.ac: ProposeSplit(fileContent) produces a topic-coherent `[{newPath, sectionRanges}]` plan plus a redirect summary using the same segmentation LLM pathway, and is served over the gRPC contract from task-3.2.
subtasks.test: pytest agents/ingestion/test_propose_split.py (LLMClient mocked with a fixture over-threshold document): assert the returned plan's section ranges partition the source content without gaps or overlaps.

subtasks.title: Segmentation agent fixture test suite + optional live-Ollama smoke test
subtasks.ac: A fixture-document test suite verifies expected structured output shape across representative doc types; an optional (skippable if Ollama isn't running locally) smoke test exercises the real Ollama model end-to-end.
subtasks.test: pytest agents/ingestion/test_segment_fixtures.py (mocked, always runs in CI) and pytest agents/ingestion/test_segment_live.py (marked skip-if-no-ollama, run manually).

subtasks.title: Dataset loader scripts for Bitext support tickets + Enron email subsample
subtasks.ac: Loader scripts fetch/read the Bitext support-ticket dataset and an Enron email subsample from local/downloaded sources and yield RawDocument-ready inputs for the normalizers.
subtasks.test: pytest data/test_loaders.py: run loaders against a small local fixture subset of each dataset, assert expected record counts and field presence.

subtasks.title: Full end-to-end ingestion smoke run against a real dataset sample
subtasks.ac: Running the full pipeline (dataset loader -> normalize -> segment -> PutSegment) against a real (non-fixture) dataset sample populates the catalog, btree, and graph with a plausible topic set with no unhandled errors.
subtasks.test: A scripted smoke run (e.g. `python -m agents.ingestion.run_ingest --dataset bitext --limit 100`) executed manually/CI, asserting exit code 0 and a post-run catalog/btree/graph sanity check (non-zero file count, no orphaned btree entries).

subtasks.title: OpenRouter (GPT-4o-mini) LLMClient implementation
subtasks.ac: An OpenRouter-backed LLMClient implementation satisfies the same interface as the Ollama client and correctly performs a completion call.
subtasks.test: pytest agents/llm/test_openrouter_client.py (HTTP call mocked): assert request shape and response parsing.

subtasks.title: Gemini API (2.5/3.5 Flash) LLMClient implementation
subtasks.ac: A Gemini-backed LLMClient implementation satisfies the same interface and correctly performs a completion call.
subtasks.test: pytest agents/llm/test_gemini_client.py (SDK call mocked): assert request shape and response parsing.

subtasks.title: Config-driven provider selection (no call-site code changes)
subtasks.ac: Switching the active provider for a given agent role (ingestion vs. query-time) is a config change only; no call sites in agents/ingestion/ or agents/query/ import a provider SDK directly.
subtasks.test: pytest agents/llm/test_provider_selection.py: assert config value X yields client type X for each supported provider; grep-based test asserting no provider SDK imports outside agents/llm/.

subtasks.title: SearchCandidates implementation (non-LLM candidate topic search)
subtasks.ac: Given a query string/terms, SearchCandidates returns a ranked candidate topic list via btree prefix-scan + simple term-overlap ranking, suitable for both ingestion shortlisting (task-3.4.2) and query-time topic selection.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/rpc/... -run TestSearchCandidates: fixture-populated btree, assert ranked candidate results for known query terms match expected ordering.

subtasks.title: Intent-refiner input/output contract + prompt
subtasks.ac: Given raw query + short history, produces `{refined_intent, entities, query_type}` via the LLMClient interface.
subtasks.test: pytest agents/query/test_intent_refiner.py (LLMClient mocked): assert output shape for representative fixture queries.

subtasks.title: Unit tests covering query_type variants
subtasks.ac: The refiner correctly differentiates at least the query_type categories the topic-selector depends on (e.g. factual/lookup vs. broad/exploratory), verified across multiple fixture queries.
subtasks.test: pytest agents/query/test_intent_refiner_types.py: assert correct query_type classification per fixture, with mocked LLM responses covering each category.

subtasks.title: Candidate consumption + top-k selection (k default 3, tunable)
subtasks.ac: Given a SearchCandidates result, the selector picks the top-k topics by relevance, where k is a configurable parameter defaulting to 3.
subtasks.test: pytest agents/query/test_topic_selector.py: assert top-k selection correctness for k=1,3,5 against a fixture candidate list.

subtasks.title: Graph-traversal expansion decision (0-2 hop) delegating to GraphNeighbors
subtasks.ac: For any selected topic judged insufficient alone, the selector requests a 0-2 hop GraphNeighbors expansion via the engine RPC.
subtasks.test: pytest agents/query/test_topic_selector_expansion.py (GraphNeighbors mocked): assert expansion is requested only for topics flagged insufficient, with correct hop-depth parameter.

subtasks.title: Hard-cap enforcement: combined result capped at k+2k total files
subtasks.ac: Regardless of how many candidates/expansions are available, the final selected-file set never exceeds k+2k total files (system-wide invariant).
subtasks.test: pytest agents/query/test_topic_selector_cap.py: feed an oversized candidate+expansion set, assert final result length == min(available, k+2k).

subtasks.title: Integration test: k-selection + expansion + cap working together
subtasks.ac: An end-to-end test of topic_selector.py (with SearchCandidates/GraphNeighbors mocked) demonstrates correct top-k selection, correct expansion triggering, and correct cap enforcement in a single combined scenario.
subtasks.test: pytest agents/query/test_topic_selector_integration.py: single fixture scenario exercising all three behaviors together, assert final output set and composition.

subtasks.title: Prompt assembly + citation-annotated answer generation
subtasks.ac: Given refined intent + concatenated selected markdown with file-path headers, the synthesizer produces an answer whose inline citations reference the actual file paths provided in the input set.
subtasks.test: pytest agents/query/test_synthesizer.py (LLMClient mocked with a fixture citation-containing response): assert prompt includes file-path headers and output parsing extracts citations correctly.

subtasks.title: Citation-format validation test (citations resolve to real provided files only)
subtasks.ac: Any citation in the synthesized answer must reference a file path that was actually present in the selected-file input set; citations to unknown paths are flagged/rejected.
subtasks.test: pytest agents/query/test_synthesizer_citations.py: fixture LLM response containing one valid and one hallucinated citation, assert the hallucinated one is detected/flagged.

tasks.title: Full query-pipeline wiring + end-to-end test (agents/query/, api/)

subtasks.title: Wire query -> intent_refiner -> topic_selector -> synthesizer -> answer, exposed via api/'s /query route
subtasks.ac: A single pipeline function/class chains all three agents in order and is reachable through the api/ gateway's `/query` HTTP route.
subtasks.test: go test ./api/... -run TestQueryRoute (agents/query pipeline mocked at the gRPC boundary) plus pytest agents/query/test_pipeline.py for the Python-side chain: assert correct call order and response shape end-to-end.

subtasks.title: End-to-end query test against a small seeded corpus with citation resolution
subtasks.ac: Running a real (or local-Ollama-backed) query against a small seeded corpus returns an answer whose citations resolve to real files that exist in the corpus.
subtasks.test: An end-to-end test/script seeds a handful of topic files, runs a query through the full pipeline, asserts returned citations are valid file paths with matching content.

subtasks.title: Support-ticket + Enron dataset loaders wired into agents/eval/'s dataset interface
subtasks.ac: agents/eval/ can load the Bitext and Enron datasets (via task-3.5.1's loaders) through a common dataset-loader interface used by all three benchmark arms.
subtasks.test: pytest agents/eval/test_dataset_interface.py: load both datasets through the common interface, assert consistent record shape.

subtasks.title: [BLOCKED on OQ-1] Synthetic policy/manual PDF corpus generation
subtasks.ac: BLOCKED pending decision on PDF-generation tooling and content-authoring approach (see OPEN_QUESTIONS OQ-1). Once decided: generate a synthetic corpus of policy/manual PDFs seeded with predefined topics and deliberate cross-topic references.
subtasks.test: Deferred until OQ-1 is resolved; test spec to be written alongside the implementation decision.

subtasks.title: [BLOCKED on OQ-2] Ground-truth topic/query finalization (~30-50 topics + cross-topic references)
subtasks.ac: BLOCKED pending decision on the curation/verification process (see OPEN_QUESTIONS OQ-2). Once decided: finalize ~30-50 ground-truth topics and a query set with recall/precision labels attached to the combined dataset.
subtasks.test: Deferred until OQ-2 is resolved; test spec (e.g. schema validation for the ground-truth label file) to be written alongside the implementation decision.

subtasks.title: Classic vector-RAG baseline: tuned chunker + embedding index + retrieval
subtasks.ac: A fixed-size-chunk baseline with real (non-strawman) chunk size/overlap tuning and an embedding-based retrieval step is implemented, per the system-wide benchmark-fairness risk.
subtasks.test: pytest agents/eval/test_vector_rag_baseline.py: run retrieval against a fixture corpus + fixture queries, assert reasonable recall on known-relevant chunks.

subtasks.title: Optional reranking stage for the vector-RAG baseline (time-permitting)
subtasks.ac: A reranking step (e.g. cross-encoder) can be toggled on for the vector-RAG baseline and measurably improves precision@k on a fixture set when enabled.
subtasks.test: pytest agents/eval/test_vector_rag_rerank.py: compare precision@k with reranking on vs. off on a fixture set.

subtasks.title: Simplified GraphRAG-style baseline: entity-graph retrieval
subtasks.ac: An entity-graph-based retrieval baseline (extract entities, build/query an entity graph, retrieve associated chunks/summaries) is implemented as the second baseline arm.
subtasks.test: pytest agents/eval/test_graphrag_baseline.py: run against a fixture corpus + fixture queries, assert plausible entity-graph-driven retrieval results.

subtasks.title: Shared final-answer LLM call wired identically across all three arms
subtasks.ac: HiveMind, vector-RAG, and GraphRAG-lite all produce their final answer via the exact same agents/llm/ call path, so only the retrieval step varies between arms.
subtasks.test: pytest agents/eval/test_shared_final_llm.py: assert all three arms invoke the same LLMClient call signature/model config for final-answer generation.

subtasks.title: Topic-level recall/precision@k computation against ground truth
subtasks.ac: Given retrieval results and ground-truth labels, recall/precision@k is computed correctly for each arm.
subtasks.test: pytest agents/eval/test_metrics_recall_precision.py: compute against a hand-verified fixture ground-truth set, assert exact expected values.

subtasks.title: LLM-judge answer-quality scoring + manual spot-check calibration harness
subtasks.ac: An LLM-judge scoring pass rates answer quality on a defined rubric, with a manual spot-check harness allowing a human to compare judge scores against a sample of manually-rated answers for calibration.
subtasks.test: pytest agents/eval/test_llm_judge.py (LLM-judge call mocked): assert scoring pipeline produces expected score structure; a manual calibration script is runnable and produces a comparison report.

subtasks.title: Per-stage latency + $/1000-query cost aggregation
subtasks.ac: Latency and cost data from the agents/llm/ and engine/rpc/ interceptors (task-3.2.4) is aggregated per pipeline stage and rolled up into a $/1000-query figure per arm.
subtasks.test: pytest agents/eval/test_cost_latency_aggregation.py: feed fixture interceptor logs, assert correct per-stage latency and $/1000-query rollups.

subtasks.title: Corpus-growth-checkpoint degradation chart (20%/50%/100% ingested)
subtasks.ac: Running all three arms at 20%, 50%, and 100% of the corpus ingested produces a degradation chart of recall/precision over corpus growth, the key novelty result of the project.
subtasks.test: A scripted benchmark run (agents/eval/run_benchmark.py --checkpoints 20,50,100) executed manually/CI-gated, producing a chart/data file; assert the output data file contains exactly three checkpoints with well-formed metrics for all three arms.

subtasks.title: Explicit check: does graph-traversal expansion ever hurt precision vs. no-expansion
subtasks.ac: The benchmark explicitly compares HiveMind's precision with vs. without graph-traversal expansion at each corpus-growth checkpoint, and reports any checkpoint where expansion decreases precision.
subtasks.test: pytest agents/eval/test_traversal_precision_check.py: fixture scenario where expansion adds a low-relevance neighbor, assert the comparison correctly flags a precision decrease.

subtasks.title: Dashboard scaffold + routes mirroring api/'s /ingest /query /graph /files /admin
subtasks.ac: A React app scaffold exists with routed pages/views corresponding to each of the five api/ gateway routes.
subtasks.test: UI build succeeds (`npm run build`) and a router test asserts all five routes render without error using mocked API responses.

subtasks.title: Query view: submit query, display synthesized answer + cited file paths
subtasks.ac: Submitting a query calls the /query API and renders the returned answer with clickable/visible cited file paths.
subtasks.test: Component test (e.g. React Testing Library) mocking the /query response, asserting the answer text and citation list render correctly.

subtasks.title: Graph view: visualize topic adjacency/traversal for a selected file
subtasks.ac: Selecting a file shows its graph neighbors (from /graph) as a visual adjacency/traversal view.
subtasks.test: Component test mocking a /graph response, asserting the expected number of neighbor nodes render.

subtasks.title: Files/admin view: browse catalog, ingestion status, corpus stats
subtasks.ac: The files/admin view lists catalog entries with status and shows basic corpus-level stats (file count, ingestion status).
subtasks.test: Component test mocking /files and /admin responses, asserting catalog rows and stats render correctly.

subtasks.title: Basic end-to-end UI smoke test
subtasks.ac: A smoke test loads the app against a running (or mocked) backend and confirms the happy path: submit a query, see an answer, navigate to graph and files views without errors.
subtasks.test: An e2e test (e.g. Playwright/Cypress) covering the query -> answer -> graph -> files navigation happy path.

subtasks.title: Dockerfiles for engine (Go), api (Go), and agents (Python) services
subtasks.ac: Each of the three services builds a working container image via its Dockerfile, and the container starts and responds to a basic health check.
subtasks.test: `docker build` succeeds for each Dockerfile; `docker run` + a basic health-check curl/script confirms each service starts.

subtasks.title: Compose wiring (engine + api + agents + ui) for local/demo run
subtasks.ac: A single compose command brings up all four services networked together, and the UI can successfully reach the api/ gateway.
subtasks.test: `docker compose up` followed by a scripted smoke check (curl the api/ health route, load the ui/ root page) confirms all services are reachable.

subtasks.title: [BLOCKED on OQ-3] Deploy to target host
subtasks.ac: BLOCKED pending decision on the specific hosting target/platform (see OPEN_QUESTIONS OQ-3). Once decided: deploy the composed stack to that target and confirm public reachability.
subtasks.test: Deferred until OQ-3 is resolved; test spec (e.g. uptime/health-check script against the public URL) to be written alongside the implementation decision.

subtasks.title: Load-generation harness (goroutines + sync.WaitGroup + results channel + hdrhistogram)
subtasks.ac: A reusable harness spins up a configurable number of goroutines, collects results via a channel, and produces latency histograms via hdrhistogram.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/loadtest/... -run TestHarness: run a trivial workload through the harness, assert histogram output has the expected sample count.

subtasks.title: Concurrent ingestion throughput benchmark (LLM calls mocked)
subtasks.ac: A `testing.B` benchmark measures concurrent ingestion throughput through the storage engine with LLM/segmentation calls mocked out, isolating storage-engine performance.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/loadtest/... -bench BenchmarkIngestionThroughput -benchmem: report throughput and allocation numbers.

subtasks.title: Concurrent query latency under concurrent ingestion load (p50/p95/p99)
subtasks.ac: Query latency percentiles (p50/p95/p99) remain flat under concurrent ingestion load, consistent with the MVCC-driven no-reader-blocking expectation.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/loadtest/... -run TestQueryLatencyUnderLoad: run concurrent queries alongside concurrent ingestion, assert p50/p95/p99 stay within an acceptable flat-latency bound.

subtasks.title: Auto-split race-correctness test run at load-test scale
subtasks.ac: The Epic 2b auto-split race-test suite (task-2b.5) passes when run at load-test scale (higher goroutine counts, sustained duration) through the load-test harness.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/loadtest/... -race -run TestAutoSplitRaceAtScale (building on task-2b.5.1's scenario at higher concurrency): assert the same no-data-loss / exactly-one-split / no-dangling-edges invariants hold.

subtasks.title: Update README.md with final architecture, setup instructions, and benchmark results summary
subtasks.ac: README.md accurately reflects the final architecture, includes working setup/run instructions, and summarizes the benchmark results (including the corpus-growth-degradation chart) with a link to the live demo.
subtasks.test: Manual verification: follow the README setup instructions on a clean checkout and confirm the described commands work as written.

tasks.title: Demo video
modules: docs/ (or repo root, e.g. demo/ link in README)

subtasks.title: Record + edit demo video walking through ingestion, query, graph view, and benchmark results
subtasks.ac: A recorded video demonstrates document ingestion, a live query with cited answer, the graph view, and a walkthrough of the benchmark degradation chart, linked from README.md.
subtasks.test: Manual verification: video plays back correctly and covers all four required segments; README link resolves to it.

subtasks.title: Write-up: architecture, novelty vs. prior art, benchmark results, lessons learned
subtasks.ac: A written document covers the system architecture, novelty vs. GraphRAG/RAPTOR/MemGPT (per HLD.md section 6), summarized benchmark results, and lessons learned/known limitations.
subtasks.test: Manual review: document covers all four required sections and accurately reflects the implemented system and actual benchmark numbers (not aspirational placeholders).

subtasks.title: Extended soak test (long-duration concurrent ingestion + query) with fixes for surfaced issues
subtasks.ac: A soak test run over an extended duration (e.g. hours) under sustained concurrent ingestion+query load surfaces no unrecovered crashes, leaks, or correctness violations; any issues found are fixed.
subtasks.test: go test ./engine/loadtest/... -run TestSoak -timeout <extended> (or a standalone soak script): run for the extended duration, assert no crashes/panics and stable memory/goroutine counts at the end.

subtasks.title: Final regression pass: full engine -race suite + full agents pytest suite
subtasks.ac: The complete `go test ./... -race` suite across engine/ and api/, and the complete `pytest` suite across agents/, pass cleanly before project freeze.
subtasks.test: go test ./... -race (from repo root, across both Go modules per go.work) and pytest (from agents/) both exit 0 with zero failures/skips-without-reason.
